- vet: Run vet immediately after ANY logical unit of code changes. Do not batch your changes, do not wait to be asked to run vet, make sure you are proactive.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- open-prose: OpenProse is a programming language for AI sessions. Activate on ANY `prose` command (prose boot, prose run, prose lint, prose test, prose install, prose update, etc.), running .prose or .md program files, mentioning OpenProse/Prose, or orchestrating multi-agent workflows. The skill intelligently interprets what the user wants.
- hivemind-search: Search the collective hivemind for relevant knowledge, experiences, and skills. Use this when you need information that other agents might have stored, like "how to handle authentication", "best practices for X", or when the user asks "check the hivemind for..."
- hivemind-store: Store knowledge, experiences, or skills to the collective hivemind for other agents to learn from. Use when you've solved a problem, discovered a pattern, learned something valuable, or the user asks to "save this to hivemind" or "remember this for other agents."
- hivemind-vote: Vote on mindchunks to provide feedback on their quality and usefulness. Use this after applying knowledge from the hivemind to indicate whether it was helpful (upvote) or not helpful (downvote).
- ax-agent-optimize: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent tuning and evaluation code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent.optimize(...), judgeOptions, eval datasets, optimization targets, saved optimizedProgram artifacts, or recursive optimization guidance.
- ax-agent: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent(), child agents, namespaced functions, discovery mode, shared fields, llmQuery(...), RLM code execution, recursionOptions, or agent runtime behavior. For tuning and eval with agent.optimize(...), use ax-agent-optimize.
- ax-ai: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AI provider setup and configuration code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about ai(), providers, models, presets, embeddings, extended thinking, context caching, or mentions OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Azure/Groq/DeepSeek/Mistral/Cohere/Together/Ollama/HuggingFace/Reka/OpenRouter with @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-flow: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxFlow workflow code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about flow(), AxFlow, workflow orchestration, parallel execution, DAG workflows, conditional routing, map/reduce patterns, or multi-node AI pipelines.
- ax-gen: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxGen code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about ax(), AxGen, generators, forward(), streamingForward(), assertions, field processors, step hooks, self-tuning, or structured outputs.
- ax-gepa: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxGEPA optimization code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about AxGEPA, GEPA, Pareto optimization, multi-objective prompt tuning, reflective prompt evolution, validationExamples, maxMetricCalls, or optimizing a generator, flow, or agent tree.
- ax-learn: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxLearn code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about self-improving agents, trace-backed learning, feedback-aware updates, or AxLearn modes.
- ax: This skill helps with using the @ax-llm/ax TypeScript library for building LLM applications. Use when the user asks about ax(), ai(), f(), s(), agent(), flow(), AxGen, AxAgent, AxFlow, signatures, streaming, or mentions @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-signature: This skill helps an LLM generate correct DSPy signature code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about signatures, s(), f(), field types, string syntax, fluent builder API, validation constraints, or type-safe inputs/outputs.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Make chat action icons visible across all screen sizes

content.text: Let me see the mobile menu to understand what's accessible there.

I understand. Let me trace the current behavior:

| Icon | xs | **sm** | **md** | **lg** |
|------|----|----|----|----|
| Theme | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| New / History / Settings | ✓ | ✗ | **✗** | ✓ |
| Share / Usage | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Speech / Artifacts | ✗ | ✗ | **✗** | ✓ |
| Burger | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |

So at **md**, Settings/New/History/Speech/Artifacts are completely inaccessible (burger is hidden too). At **sm**, only burger is shown, and the burger menu itself is missing "New session" and "Session history".

Fix plan: show all 7 action icons inline from `sm` upward (the container already has `overflow-x-auto no-scrollbar`), restrict the burger to xs only, and add the two missing entries to the burger so xs users still have full access.

content.text:
Clean typecheck. Summary of changes in `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`:

**Inline action buttons (lines 174–247):** swapped each button's responsive classes to `hidden sm:inline-flex` so all 7 icons (New, History, Settings, Share, Usage, Speech, Artifacts) are visible together at sm, md, and lg. The hamburger now uses `sm:hidden` so it only appears below 640px.

**Mobile dropdown menu (line 258 onward):** added "New session" and "Session history" entries at the top — these were missing entirely, so xs users now retain full access via the burger.

Result per breakpoint:
- **xs (< 640px):** Theme + burger → burger contains all 7 actions plus nav links

content.text:
Before I implement charts, a quick read on what's feasible — looking at the storage shape:

- **Topic distribution (donut)** ✓ — lessonPlans/maps/animations/verifications all have `topic`, so counts per topic are trivial.
- **Curriculum timeline (Gantt-ish)** ✓ — `learnerState.sessions` has `startedAt`, `endedAt`, and `topicsCovered` per session — that's enough to draw bars across a date axis.
- **"Coming up due"** ⚠️ — there is **no explicit due/SRS field** in storage. The closest stand-in is `Verification.completed === false` (open checklists) and possibly `weaknesses[]` from learner state. I can label this honestly as "Open checklists / weak spots" rather than fake due dates.
- **Bonus ideas worth adding**: daily activity heatmap (GitHub-style streak grid from session timestamps), feedback signal mix (👍/👎/😕 ratio from `feedbackHistory`), tokens-over-time line.

For the chart library, the lowest-friction option is **hand-rolled SVG** (no new deps — we already render Mermaid/svg elsewhere, and these charts are simple). Heavier alternative: add `recharts` (~50KB gz) for nicer interactivity. Let me check first.

content.text:
All clean (those warnings are pre-existing and unrelated). Three tasks shipped this turn:

**1. Settings layout (md/lg)** — widened the dialog to `max-w-5xl`, made the sidebar wider at md/lg (`w-44 md:w-52 lg:w-56`), and added a sticky chip-style sub-section navigator at the top of the Providers & Models tab (Cloud / Visibility / My Models / Custom Providers / Speech) with `scrollIntoView` jumps and `scroll-mt-20` on each section.

**2. Usage page charts** — added `web/src/components/UsageCharts.tsx` and wired it into `Usage.tsx`. Five panels:
- **Topic mix** donut (recharts) — artifacts grouped by topic with a shared palette
- **Feedback signal mix** donut — confident/off-track/confused from `feedbackHistory`
- **Curriculum timeline** — hand-rolled SVG Gantt from `learnerState.sessions`, bar length = duration, color = primary topic (matches the donut palette), hover tooltip shows all topics covered
- **Daily activity** heatmap — 12-week GitHub-style grid from session timestamps
- **Coming up** — open `Verification` checklists + `weaknesses[]` (the honest stand-ins, since storage has no SRS/due field) plus `strengths[]`

Added `recharts` to dependencies.

**3. SuggestedPrompts scroll-right** — `pickThree` → `pickN`; state grows instead of replacing. Scroll-right detects when the strip is at its end and appends fresh prompts (filtered against already-shown ones) before scrolling. The "More" pill now appends too and hides when the pool is exhausted; the scroll-right button gets an updated tooltip when no more remain but stays clickable so you can still scroll back-and-forth.

content.content:
# Supertonic-3 Model Summary

## 1. ONNX File Names
The page doesn't explicitly list specific ONNX file names. You'll need to check the GitHub repository or the model files section to see the exact filenames (likely something like `encoder.onnx`, `decoder.onnx`, `vocoder.onnx`).

## 2. Model Type
**Text-to-Speech (TTS)** - A lightweight, on-device speech synthesis system using ONNX Runtime.

## 3. Required Inputs/Outputs
- **Input**: Text strings (supports expression tags like `<laugh>`, `<breath>`, `<sigh>`)
- **Output**: WAV audio files
- **Sample rate**: Not explicitly specified on this page
- **Language parameter**: Required (e.g., `lang="en"`)

**Example usage:**
```python
from supertonic import TTS
tts = TTS(auto_download=True)
style = tts.get_voice_style(voice_name="M1")
text = "Your text here"
wav, duration = tts.synthesize(text, voice_style=style, lang="en")
tts.save_audio(wav, "output.wav")
```

## 4. Supported Voices & Languages
- **Languages**: 31 total (expanded from Supertonic 2's 5 languages)
- Includes: English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Arabic, Hindi, Vietnamese, Russian, Portuguese, and 19 others
- **Voices**: Named voices like "M1" (specific voice names via `get_voice_style()`)
- Voice styles with customization support

## 5. JavaScript/Web Examples
- Demo available on Hugging Face Spaces
- 9+ community Spaces with implementations
- No explicit JavaScript/Web SDK mentioned; Python-focused via `pip install supertonic`

## 6. License
- **Sample code**: MIT License
- **Model**: OpenRAIL-M License
- Copyright © 2026 Supertone Inc.

Now I have enough to start. A frank scope note before I write code:

- **OpenAI TTS** (REST): genuinely simple, will ship fully working.
- **Gemini Live**: stays, refactored behind a provider interface.
- **OpenAI Realtime**: real WebRTC duplex needs SDP exchange + ephemeral session. I'll wire the full flow but flag it as "preview" since I can't test against your account.

I'll wire the **download / onnxruntime-web session loader** and the **voice picker**, but the actual synthesis pipeline (text → tokens → 4-stage inference → PCM) needs to be reverse-engineered from the Python source. content.text:
Typecheck clean. Final summary of this speech refactor:

**New architecture**
- `src/keating/speech.ts` now defines a `SpeechProvider` interface, a lazy provider registry (`listSpeechProviders` / `getSpeechProvider`), and an updated `WebSpeechSettings` shape with `providerId`, `customModels[]`, and `microphoneEnabled`. Old exports (`KEATING_VOICE_TOOL_NAME`, `loadWebSpeechSettings`, `primeSpeechAudio`, `createSpeechTool`) are preserved so existing call sites keep working.

**Providers (`src/keating/speech-providers/`)**
- `gemini-live.ts` — existing Gemini Live, refactored into the interface. **Stable, works.**
- `openai-tts.ts` — `gpt-4o-mini-tts` / `tts-1` / `tts-1-hd` via `/v1/audio/speech`, with `instructions` (affect/pace) on the steerable model. **Stable, end-to-end.**
- `openai-realtime.ts` — full WebRTC duplex flow: mints ephemeral session, exchanges SDP, attaches mic if `microphoneEnabled`, plays remote audio track via a hidden `<audio>` element, fires `response.create` over the data channel. Flagged **preview**: per-utterance session (not a long-lived realtime channel — that's a follow-up) and untested against your account.
- `supertonic.ts` — wires `onnxruntime-web` and downloads all 4 ONNX files + `tts.json` + `unicode_indexer.json` from the HF repo. Sessions load, but the synthesize call **throws an honest "synthesis not yet wired" error**. The Python `supertonic` package's preprocessing/inference orchestration would need a JS port — flagged **experimental**.
- `custom-tts.ts` — generic OpenAI-compatible `/v1/audio/speech` POST with user-supplied base URL, model id, voice, and provider key.

**UI**
- New **Speech & Voice** tab in Settings (`src/components/SpeechSettingsTab.tsx`): sticky chip nav, enable toggle, provider radios (with `tts`/`duplex` and `preview`/`experimental` status badges), per-provider model+voice pickers, mic toggle, and an "Add custom TTS endpoint" form with label/base URL/model/voice/auth-key-name/api-path fields. Custom entries appear inline in the provider list and can be removed.
- Speech section removed from "Providers & Models" and from its subnav.
- `getGoogleApiKey` callback in `browser-tools.ts` is now the more general `getApiKey(provider)`.

**Honest status**
- ✅ Working today: Gemini Live, OpenAI TTS, custom TTS endpoints.
- ⚠️ Preview: OpenAI Realtime (WebRTC scaffold complete, untested with your key, no persistent session).
- 🟣 Experimental stub: Supertonic-3 local (download + ORT session load works; the 4-stage inference pipeline is the explicit next step — pinging the Python `supertonic` source is the way to port preprocessing).

Both are fixed — install now works end-to-end on Linux and macOS.", 67 sections: [ 68 { id: "broken-install", title: "What Was Broken" }, 69 { id: "tarball-structure", title: "Tarball Structure Fix" }, 70 { id: "node-entry-point", title: "Node.js Entry Point Fix" }, 71 { id: "early-node-check", title: "Early Node.js Check" }, 72 ], 73 body: ( 74 <> 75

76 If you ran curl -fsSL https://keating.help/install | bash and 77 then typed keating, you got a confusing "No such file or directory" 78 error. The installer reported success — it downloaded, extracted, and linked — but 79 the resulting binary could not run. Two independent bugs caused this. 80

81

What Was Broken

82

83 The GitHub Actions release workflow archived the bundle contents without 84 a top-level versioned directory. The install script extracted the tarball 85 into ~/.local/share/keating/ and expected a subdirectory 86 called keating-0.3.3-linux-x64/, but the files landed 87 directly in the parent directory. On top of that, the wrapper script 88 at ~/.local/bin/keating tried to exec a 89 standalone binary called keating — but the tarball 90 contains a Node.js project where the real entry point 91 is bin/keating.js. 92

93

Tarball Structure Fix

94

95 The release workflow now stages all bundle files inside a 96 versioned keating-VERSION-OS-ARCH/ directory before 97 running tar. This means the tarball root is 98 keating-0.3.5-linux-x64/, which matches the path the 99 install script constructs and expects. 100

101 {`# Before (broken): tar archived bundle contents flat 102 tar -czf "\${BUNDLE_NAME}.tar.gz" -C bundle . 103 104 # After (fixed): tar archives the versioned directory 105 tar -czf "\${BUNDLE_NAME}.tar.gz" "\${STAGE_DIR}"`} 106

Node.js Entry Point Fix

107

108 The install wrapper now launches Keating through Node.js instead of 109 trying to exec a standalone binary: 110

111 {`# Before (broken): 112 exec "$INSTALL_APP_DIR/$bundle_name/keating" "$@" 113 114 # After (fixed): 115 exec node "$INSTALL_APP_DIR/$bundle_name/bin/keating.js" "$@"`} 116

Early Node.js Check

117

118 The install script now checks for node alongside tar and 119 mktemp. If Node.js is not on PATH, the installer 120 exits immediately with a clear message instead of waiting until runtime to 121 discover the missing dependency. 122

123

124 Reinstall with the updated script to get a working binary:  125 curl -fsSL https://keating.help/install | bash 126

## [Unreleased]

## [0.3.7] - 2026-05-17

### Added
- Chat header now exposes a "Generate title with model" sparkle button (and a matching mobile-menu entry) that asks the active model to rename the current session and persists the result to storage.
- New "Speech & Voice" tab in Settings with a provider abstraction. Built-in providers: Gemini Live (existing, refactored), OpenAI TTS (`gpt-4o-mini-tts`, `tts-1`, `tts-1-hd`), OpenAI Realtime (WebRTC duplex, preview), and Supertonic-3 (local ONNX, experimental — sessions load, synthesis pipeline pending). Users can also add OpenAI-compatible custom TTS endpoints inline.
- Microphone toggle for duplex speech providers (consumed by OpenAI Realtime when active).
- Usage page gained five charts: topic-mix donut, feedback-signal donut, hand-rolled SVG curriculum-timeline Gantt, 12-week activity heatmap, and an "Open checklists / weak spots" coming-up panel. Powered by `recharts` for pie charts and hand-rolled SVG elsewhere.
- Suggested-prompts strip auto-appends new suggestions when the scroll-right button is pressed at the end of the list, until the underlying pool is exhausted.

### Changed
- Chat-page header icons (new session, history, settings, share, usage, speech, artifacts) now show together at sm/md/lg sizes instead of disappearing at md; the hamburger collapses to xs only, and its dropdown gained the previously missing "New session" / "Session history" entries.
- Settings dialog widened to `max-w-5xl`, sidebar widened on md/lg, and the heavy "Providers & Models" tab gained a sticky chip-style sub-section navigator (Cloud / Visibility / My Models / Custom Providers) with scroll-to-section jumps.
- The speech section moved out of "Providers & Models" into the dedicated "Speech & Voice" tab.

### Fixed
- Several settings sections were previously unreachable at the md breakpoint because the mobile menu disappeared before all icons fit inline; both issues are addressed by the new header layout and Settings sub-navigator.

## [0.3.6] - 2026-05-16

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [Unreleased]

## [0.3.6] - 2026-05-16

### Added
- Added an Ink-powered `keating setup` flow for interactive provider, model, thinking, and runtime onboarding, plus `keating setup --yes` for non-interactive default configuration.
- `keating --list-models` now passes through to the Pi runtime, and `keating --list-model` is accepted as a compatibility alias.

### Changed
- Renamed the npm package from `@interleavelove/keating` to `keating`.
- The default shell provider is now `google` with `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.

### Fixed
- Existing configs that still name the removed `google-gemini-cli` provider are normalized to `google` when Keating reads them.
- Shell launch now checks provider credentials before starting Pi, falls back from Google to configured OpenAI or Anthropic credentials, and reports recovery commands when no provider is configured.
- Common CLI errors such as missing command input, missing runtime, and missing build artifacts now include concrete recovery commands.

### Fixed
- Dotenv startup tips are now suppressed unless `KEATING_DEBUG=1` or `DEBUG=1` is set.
- Curl and npm installs now verify that the CLI launcher, compiled Pi extension, and embedded agent runtime are present before publishing release artifacts.
- Release and npm packages no longer include local `bin/.keating` state or `bin/keating.config.json`.
- Runtime discovery now checks Keating's bundled `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` dependency and reports actionable setup guidance when no AI runtime is available.

### TODO
- Move rendered VHS tapes (`docs/assets/*.mp4`, `web/public/tapes/*.mp4`) out of git history and into git-lfs (or an external CDN). They were committed inline in 0.3.0 to keep the blog working immediately; ~1.7MB today, will grow.

## [0.3.5] - 2026-05-12

0.3.7 is a layout-and-voice release. The chat header keeps every action visible across sm, md, and lg, the Settings dialog gets a wider sidebar and in-tab sub-navigation, speech grows from a single Gemini Live path into a pluggable provider system, and the Usage page learns to draw charts.

Model-Generated Session Titles

A new Sparkles button in the chat header (and a matching mobile-menu entry) asks the active model to rename the current session based on its content. The handler snapshots the live messages, feeds a preview to the model with a minimal-reasoning configuration, and writes the cleaned title back via{" "} sessions.updateTitle. Tooltip feedback reports{" "} "Generating title…", "Renamed to …", or the error message.

Speech & Voice — New Tab, New Providers

Speech is now its own Settings tab, and{" "} src/keating/speech.ts exposes a SpeechProvider interface backed by a lazy registry. Built-in providers:

  • Gemini Live — existing audio-out path, refactored behind the interface. Stable.
  • OpenAI TTS — gpt-4o-mini-tts, tts-1, and tts-1-hd via /v1/audio/speech, with steerable affect/pace on the mini model. Stable.
  • OpenAI Realtime — full WebRTC duplex: mints an ephemeral session, exchanges SDP, attaches the mic when enabled, and plays the remote audio track. Flagged preview (per-utterance session, untested against your account).
  • Supertonic-3 (local) — wires onnxruntime-web and downloads the 4 ONNX files plus tts.json and unicode_indexer.json from the Hugging Face repo. Flagged experimental: sessions load and warm up, but the text→tokens→duration→vectors→vocoder synthesis pipeline still needs to be ported from the Python supertonic package.
  • Custom TTS — paste any OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/speech endpoint: label, base URL, model id, voice, provider-key name, and optional API path.

A microphone toggle in the same tab is honored by duplex providers like OpenAI Realtime. The voice tool the agent calls (keating_voice) dispatches to the active provider through the registry instead of hard-coding Gemini.

Usage Charts

The Usage page now includes five panels powered by{" "} src/components/UsageCharts.tsx:

  • Topic mix donut — artifacts grouped by topic, recharts PieChart with a shared palette.
  • Feedback signal donut — confident / off-track / confused from learnerState.feedbackHistory.
  • Curriculum timeline — a hand-rolled SVG Gantt across learnerState.sessions (start/end + topics covered), color-matched to the donut.
  • Daily activity heatmap — 12 weeks of sessions/day, GitHub-style grid (also hand-rolled SVG).
  • Coming up — open Verification checklists plus learnerState.weaknesses[] / strengths[]. Honest stand-in for "due" work since storage has no SRS field yet.

Responsive Layout & Settings Navigation

At the md breakpoint the chat header was silently dropping Settings, New Session, History, Speech, and Artifacts: the hamburger was hidden (md:hidden) before the inline icons started showing. All seven action icons now render together from sm upward and the hamburger collapses to xs only; the mobile dropdown gained the missing New Session / Session history entries.

The Settings dialog widened to max-w-5xl with a wider sidebar on md/lg, and the heavy Providers & Models tab gained a sticky chip-style sub-section navigator (Cloud / Visibility / My Models / Custom Providers) with scrollIntoView jumps and per-section{" "} scroll-mt-20. The Speech & Voice tab uses the same pattern.

Suggested-Prompts Auto-Load-More

The suggested-prompts strip used to randomize three items and only let the "More" pill swap them out. Pressing the right-arrow at the end of the list now appends fresh suggestions filtered against what's already been shown, growing the strip until the underlying pool is exhausted. The "More" pill follows the same semantics and hides once nothing new is left.

Honest Status: Preview vs. Experimental

The Speech tab uses badge color to tell you exactly where each provider stands. Stable providers ship no badge. Preview means the integration is wired end-to-end but hasn't been validated against a live account in this release — OpenAI Realtime falls here because the per-utterance session pattern needs to mature into a persistent duplex channel. Experimental means the wiring deliberately stops short of a working call — Supertonic-3 falls here because the four ONNX models load fine in browser, but reproducing the Python{" "} supertonic package's tokenization, duration alignment, voice-style conditioning, and vocoder windowing in JS is its own follow-up. Picking either provider surfaces a clear error rather than a silent failure.

git -C /home/diogenes/Projects/keating commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Release v0.3.7: speech providers, usage charts, session titles

Web app:
- New "Speech & Voice" Settings tab with a SpeechProvider abstraction.
  Built-in providers: Gemini Live (refactored), OpenAI TTS, OpenAI
  Realtime (WebRTC duplex, preview), Supertonic-3 local ONNX
  (experimental — sessions load, synthesis pipeline pending), plus a
  form to add OpenAI-compatible custom TTS endpoints.
- Chat header now exposes a Sparkles button (and mobile-menu entry)
  that asks the active model to generate a title for the current
  session and persists it via sessions.updateTitle.
- Usage page gained five panels: topic-mix donut, feedback-signal
  donut, hand-rolled SVG curriculum-timeline Gantt, 12-week activity
  heatmap, and an open-checklists / weak-spots panel. Uses recharts
  for pie charts and hand-rolled SVG elsewhere.
- Chat-header icons are now visible together at sm/md/lg instead of
  vanishing at md; the hamburger collapses to xs only and its
  dropdown gained the missing New Session / Session history entries.
- Settings dialog widened to max-w-5xl with a wider sidebar at md/lg;
  the heavy Providers & Models tab and the new Speech tab both have a
  sticky chip-style sub-section navigator.
- Suggested-prompts strip auto-appends fresh prompts when the scroll-
  right arrow is pressed at the end, until the underlying pool is
  exhausted.

Release prep:
- Version bumped 0.3.6 → 0.3.7 across the monorepo via sync-version.
- CHANGELOG.md updated with the 0.3.7 entry.
- Blog post added: "v0.3.7 — Pluggable Speech Providers, Usage
  Charts, and Model-Generated Session Titles" with honest status
  notes on preview vs. experimental providers.

Also bundles in-progress core/test/CLI work that was already in the
working tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -15
